title: Handshake vs We-Connect — Honest Comparison for LinkedIn Automation
description: Comparing Handshake and We-Connect for LinkedIn automation. See features, pricing, safety, and multi-sender support side-by-side to pick the right tool for your team.

overview.handshake: Handshake is a cloud-based LinkedIn automation platform purpose-built for multi-sender rotation. Teams manage unlimited LinkedIn sender accounts from a single dashboard with built-in warmup scheduling, a unified inbox, and premium residential proxies on every plan. Pricing is based on sender accounts — not per-seat — with unlimited team seats included. Plans start at $69/month for 1 sender and scale through Growth ($199/mo for 5 senders) and Scale ($299/mo for 10 senders) tiers.
overview.competitor: We-Connect is a cloud-based LinkedIn automation tool that positions itself as a safe, user-friendly option for professionals and teams. It offers a Growth/Starter plan at $69/seat/month and a Professional plan at $79/seat/month, with custom pricing for agencies and enterprises. We-Connect supports smart LinkedIn campaigns with automated connections, messages, profile visits, and endorsements. The Professional plan adds multichannel capabilities combining LinkedIn and email outreach, plus third-party integrations. We-Connect includes a 14-day free trial and offers annual billing discounts.

featureComparison.handshake: Built-in, core feature — rotate outreach across multiple LinkedIn accounts automatically
featureComparison.competitor: Not available — each seat manages one LinkedIn account independently

featureComparison.handshake: Automated warmup scheduling with gradual ramp-up for new accounts
featureComparison.competitor: Smart campaign safety limits, but no dedicated automated warmup feature

featureComparison.handshake: Single inbox for all sender accounts — manage every conversation centrally
featureComparison.competitor: Per-account inbox management — no cross-account unified view

featureComparison.handshake: Unlimited team seats on every plan, shared workspaces and dashboards
featureComparison.competitor: Team member management with custom permissions on Growth plan
LinkedIn + email multichannel campaigns on Professional plan ($79/seat/mo)

featureComparison.handshake: Premium residential proxies included on all plans, human-like sending patterns
featureComparison.competitor: Cloud-based with safety-focused design, but proxy details not specified

featureComparison.handshake: A/B testing, acceptance rate monitoring, reply tracking
featureComparison.competitor: Smart campaigns with unlimited LinkedIn actions and automated sequences

featureComparison.handshake: Built-in A/B testing on all plans
featureComparison.competitor: Message variations possible but no dedicated A/B testing framework

featureComparison.handshake: API and webhook integrations on all plans

featureComparison.competitor: LinkedIn Profile Optimizer add-on and Post Remix tool available

sections.handshakeWins:
Handshake and We-Connect are remarkably close on paper — both are cloud-based, both focus on LinkedIn safety, and both start around $69/month. The differences become clear when you look at scaling and team infrastructure.

Multi-sender rotation is the biggest differentiator. We-Connect charges per seat, and each seat operates one LinkedIn account independently. There's no way to distribute leads across multiple accounts or rotate outreach automatically. Handshake's entire architecture is built around this — your campaigns spread across all connected sender accounts natively.

The pricing gap is the direct consequence. At 5 senders, We-Connect costs $345-$395/month. Handshake covers 5 senders for $199/month — a 43-50% savings. At 10 senders, it's $690-$790/month vs. $299/month. And every Handshake plan includes unlimited team seats, while We-Connect charges per seat regardless of whether that person is a sender or just a manager.

Handshake explicitly includes premium residential proxies on every plan. We-Connect markets itself as safe and cloud-based but doesn't specify the type of IP infrastructure used for each account — a detail that matters significantly for LinkedIn detection avoidance.

The unified inbox across all sender accounts gives team leads and managers a single view of every conversation. We-Connect manages conversations per-account, requiring context-switching between accounts to see all replies.
sections.competitorWins:
We-Connect has strengths in multichannel outreach, profile optimization tools, and ease of use.

The Professional plan ($79/seat/mo) includes multichannel campaigns combining LinkedIn and email — something Handshake doesn't offer. For users who want both channels in one platform, We-Connect provides a streamlined experience without needing a separate email outreach tool.

We-Connect's LinkedIn Profile Optimizer add-on is a unique offering. It helps users improve their LinkedIn profiles for better acceptance rates and visibility — a feature that complements automation by addressing the profile quality that influences campaign performance.

The Post Remix tool for repurposing LinkedIn content is another unique add-on that helps users maintain organic activity alongside automated outreach — something LinkedIn's algorithm rewards.

We-Connect also has a reputation for user-friendliness. The interface is clean and intuitive, and the 14-day free trial is generous enough to thoroughly evaluate the platform before committing.
sections.whoShouldChooseHandshake: Choose Handshake if you're running outreach from multiple LinkedIn accounts and need centralized management at a predictable cost. It's ideal for sales teams with 3+ sender accounts, agencies managing client campaigns, and anyone who needs multi-sender rotation with a unified inbox. If you're currently paying We-Connect for 5+ seats and want to cut costs by 40-50% while gaining rotation capabilities, Handshake is the clear upgrade.
sections.whoShouldChooseCompetitor: Choose We-Connect if you're a solo user or small team that values multichannel LinkedIn + email outreach in a single platform. It's also a good fit if you want the Profile Optimizer and Post Remix add-ons to improve your overall LinkedIn presence alongside outreach. For users running 1-2 LinkedIn accounts who want a clean, simple interface with good safety practices, We-Connect is a solid option.
sections.verdict: At 1 sender, Handshake and We-Connect are nearly identical in price — both around $69-$79/month. The decision at that scale comes down to multichannel needs (We-Connect wins) vs. included proxies and warmup (Handshake wins). But the moment you scale beyond 2-3 accounts, Handshake's pricing advantage becomes decisive: $199/mo for 5 senders vs. $345-$395/mo, with multi-sender rotation and unified inbox included. For teams scaling LinkedIn outreach, Handshake delivers significantly more value per dollar.

faqs.question: How do Handshake and We-Connect compare on pricing?
faqs.answer: At 1 sender, they're similar ($69/mo each). The gap widens at scale: 5 senders cost $199/mo on Handshake vs. $345-$395/mo on We-Connect. At 10 senders, it's $299/mo vs. $690-$790/mo. Handshake includes unlimited team seats; We-Connect charges per seat.

faqs.question: Does We-Connect support multi-sender rotation?
faqs.answer: No. Each We-Connect seat manages one LinkedIn account independently. There's no automatic lead distribution or rotation between accounts — a core feature that Handshake provides natively.

faqs.question: Can We-Connect do email outreach?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Professional plan at $79/seat/month includes multichannel campaigns combining LinkedIn and email. Handshake focuses exclusively on LinkedIn automation.

faqs.question: Which tool is safer for LinkedIn accounts?
faqs.answer: Both are cloud-based, which is inherently safer than browser extensions. Handshake explicitly includes premium residential proxies on every plan. We-Connect emphasizes safety-focused design but doesn't specify its IP infrastructure details.

faqs.question: Can I switch from We-Connect to Handshake?
faqs.answer: Yes. Connect your LinkedIn accounts to Handshake and set up campaigns. No data migration is required. Most teams are running campaigns within a day of switching.
